scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a standard warranty repair only when proof-of-purchase, model and serial, service-order, and completion evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing purchase, serial, and service-order evidence produced a blocked-state exception instead of improvised coverage handling.
The lane refused closure without technician and customer-facing completion artifacts and preserved an auditable open state.
Booking, order, and payment mismatches stayed unresolved and were routed to correction owners rather than hidden inside a dispatch or close decision.
Safety and recall boundaries remained intact once the warranty issue intersected with a CPSC-style hazard path.
Refund and policy-exception authority was treated as out of scope and escalated cleanly.
Wrong-part and repeat-failure conditions moved into rework handling with explicit evidence and next-owner routing.
Goodwill replacement and fee-waiver authority was rejected as an adjacent-lane responsibility.
High-volume queue handling preserved realistic warranty terminology and still enforced evidence and sequencing controls.
Missing tenant authority, commercial-use, and service-plan facts triggered low-confidence escalation rather than confident synthesis.
Conflicting FTC, OEM, and tenant policy signals were recorded as a precedence conflict that required escalation.
Tenant-specific serial-photo and identity controls were applied without expanding the lane into fraud adjudication or unsupported denial authority.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
The package stays inside dealer warranty and product-service execution and does not claim refund-approval, legal, recall-command, or fraud-investigation authority.
